reasoning: HKU5-CoV-2 was discovered in bats and published in February 2025 as a merbecovirus capable of binding to human ACE2 in vitro, sparking media coverage about a potential pandemic threat. However, multiple news reports and scientific articles right up to and past July 1, 2025, definitively state that no human cases have been observed. For instance, an article from May 23, 2025 notes "As of now, HKU5-CoV-2 has not infected humans," and a June 6, 2025 article characterizes it as "only small step from infecting humans." Searches for any subsequent human infection yielded no results, and a January 2026 Wikipedia review still refers to it plainly as a bat coronavirus isolated from bats, with no history of human spillover. Therefore, 0 cases were reported before July 1, 2025.
